channel.title: Robotics on Baltic Vectors
channel.description: Recent content in Robotics on Baltic Vectors

item.title: What puts the A in VLA?
item.description:
The field of robotics is experiencing an algorithmic shift towards VLAs (Vision Language Action) models - teaching transformers to “speak” robot actions in the physical world.

In this post, I’ll:

  • Demystify robot actions - what do VLAs output to make robots move.
  • Review how action representations have evolved over the past couple of years.

I don’t have a robotics background so all of this is quite new to me, and I’ve always been miffed by the bridging of what must happen between a model outputting tokens to an actual arm flipping a pancake. What do robot actions actually look like?
